extension.summary: v3: route ISO/Blu-ray to the OPPO via playercorefactory — no Kodi pre-play blip.
extension.description: OppoKodiBridge v3 is a fork of v2 that intercepts playback with Kodi's playercorefactory instead of a service monitor. Kodi routes disc content (.iso, BDMV, VIDEO_TS) to an external player that hands the file to the OPPO/M9205 over its HTTP app API — so Kodi never starts the file itself and there is no brief "Kodi plays, then hands off" blip that v2 has. TV input switching is meant to be CEC-free via a network IR blaster (Broadlink RM4 mini); until one is configured it falls back to an interim OPPO power-cycle, and there is no CEC reclaim (v3 is CEC-free by design). It installs alongside v2 (different add-on id) so the two interception approaches can be compared directly. The OPPO HTTP API is community-reverse-engineered, not an official protocol.
extension.news: v3.0.0 — first cut of the playercorefactory fork. The service writes a playercorefactory.xml routing .iso + BDMV/VIDEO_TS to an external OPPO player (pcf_player.py), so there is no pre-play blip. Playback state is polled over HTTP /getglobalinfo; TV switch is Broadlink IR when configured (CEC-free), else an interim OPPO power-cycle with no CEC reclaim. Installs beside v2 for A/B comparison.
